channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Israeli strikes kill at least 17 in Gaza as ground troops enter Palestinian territory's north
item.description: Israeli strikes have killed more than a dozen people in the Gaza Strip as Israel sent more ground troops into the Palestinian territory to ramp up its offensive against Hamas.
item.dc:creator: Wafaa Shurafa, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Danish leader tells the US 'you cannot annex another country' as she visits Greenland
item.description: NUUK, Greenland (AP) — Denmark's prime minister has told the U.S. during a visit to Greenland that “you cannot annex another country,” even with the argument that international security is at stake. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: A week after catastrophic earthquake, focus turns to a growing humanitarian crisis in Myanmar
item.description: Search teams have pulled more bodies from the ruins of buildings a week after a massive earthquake rocked Myanmar killing more than 3,100 people.
item.dc:creator: David Rising, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: A church in England opens its doors to pro wrestling in a bid to attract converts
item.description: SHIPLEY, England (AP) — Sitting around a wrestling ring, churchgoers roared as local hero Billy O’Keeffe body-slammed a fighter named Disciple. Beneath stained-glass windows, they whooped and cheered as burly, tattooed wresters tumbled into the aisle during a six-man tag-team battle.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Haiti taking more steps against gangs after thousands of protesters demand security
item.description: PORT-AU-PRINCE, Haiti (AP) — The leader of Haiti’s transitional presidential council tried to allay surging alarm among Haitians over rampant gang violence by acknowledging Thursday that the country has “become hell for everyone” and promising new measures to stop the bloodshed.
item.dc:creator: EVENS SANON and DÁNICA COTO, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Sri Lanka's target to be free of land mines by 2028 is under threat as US reviews aid
item.description: Thavarathnam Pushparani fought on the front lines for the now-defeated Tamil Tiger rebels against the Sri Lankan forces in its decades-long separatist war and later took to clearing the land mines on the same battle lines.
item.dc:creator: Eranga Jayawardena And Krishan Francis, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Yoon Suk Yeol had a rapid rise in South Korean politics before an abrupt downfall
item.description: South Korea's Constitutional Court has upheld parliament's impeachment of President Yoon Suk Yeol and removed him from office.
item.dc:creator: Hyung-Jin Kim, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Rubio tries to reassure wary allies of US commitment to NATO as Trump sends mixed signals
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio and the Trump administration’s new envoy to NATO are seeking to reassure wary members of the U.S. commitment to the alliance. Rubio on Thursday decried “hysteria and hyperbole” in the media about U.S.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE and LORNE COOK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Slovaks rally to protest against legislation that critics say is harmful to NGOs
item.description: BRATISLAVA, Slovakia (AP) — Thousands marched through Slovakia's capital on Thursday before a planned rally in front of parliament to condemn draft legislation that critics consider detrimental for many nongovernmental organizations.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: World leaders react to Trump’s sweeping tariffs: ‘tumultuous times’ ahead
item.description: World leaders had varied reactions to President Donald Trump's tariff announcements, with many pledging counter measures, while others hope to negotiate a removal of the import taxes.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: White House defends not including Russia, North Korea on tariffs
item.description: The official said that President Trump has “recently threatened to impose strong sanctions on Russia” to further explain leaving out Moscow.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Threats, calls for negotiations in wake of Trump tariffs
item.description: Countries around the globe have responded to President Trump's latest tariffs with either threats of retaliation or calls for negotiation. Trump on Wednesday imposed a 10 percent base tax on all goods coming into the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Rubio arrives in Brussels for NATO talks amid unease over Trump’s agenda
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio arrived at in Brussels Thursday for talks that allies hope will shed light on the U.S.’ future plans in Europe.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Indian parliament's Lower House passes controversial bill that would change Muslim endowments
item.description: SRINAGAR, India (AP) — The Lower House of India's parliament early Thursday passed a controversial bill moved by Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s Hindu nationalist government to amend laws governing Muslim land endowments.
item.dc:creator: AIJAZ HUSSAIN and SHEIKH SAALIQ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The EU Parliament has transparency problems. Marine Le Pen's case is a window into what's wrong
item.description: The conviction of one of the most powerful figures of the European far right for embezzling EU Parliament funds has sent shockwaves around the continent and beyond. But Marine Le Pen's case is just one example of transparency problems that have plagued the legislature.
item.dc:creator: ELLA JOYNER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Suspected US airstrikes in Yemen kill at least 6 people, Houthi rebels say
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Suspected U.S. airstrikes battered rebel-controlled areas of Yemen into Wednesday, with the Houthis saying the attacks killed at least six people across the country.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ontario premier ‘cautiously optimistic’ amid Trump tariffs
item.description: “Make no mistake about it, Canadians love Americans. They love the U.S., and I do, too,” Doug Ford said.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: Trump tariffs likely to spur recession: Moody's economist
item.description: Mark Zandi said the situation may not be as grave if countries do not respond “in kind” to the president’s tariffs.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: Canada's PM Carney vows to ‘fight’ Trump’s tariffs, other world leaders weigh impact
item.description: President Trump's newest tariffs sparked pledges by Canada's prime minster to 'fight' back while some said they were committed to working with partners and others look to take on Washington.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israeli leader Netanyahu will visit Hungary, defying an international arrest warrant
item.description: BUDAPEST, Hungary (AP) — Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu is scheduled to arrive in Hungary on Wednesday to meet with its nationalist prime minister despite an international arrest warrant for the Israeli leader over the war in the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN SPIKE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Canada stares down consequences of Trump tariff war: Job losses, grocery price hikes, possible recession
item.description: Canadians are bracing alongside other geopolitical partners as President Donald Trump prepares to unleash his next round of tariffs in what he is calling "liberation day.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Middle East latest: Israeli defense minister says military is expanding its operation in Gaza
item.description: Israel’s military operation in the Gaza Strip is expanding to seize “large areas,” the defense minister said Wednesday, as airstrikes on the southern city of Khan Younis killed 17 people overnight, according to hospital officials.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: As Israel advances in Gaza, many exhausted families flee again. Some can't bear it
item.description: KHAN YOUNIS, Gaza Strip (AP) — As Israel orders wide new evacuations across the Gaza Strip, Palestinians say they are crushed by exhaustion and hopelessness at the prospect of fleeing once again. Many are packing a few belongings and trudging off in search of new shelters.
item.dc:creator: FATMA KHALED and MOHAMMAD JAHJOUH, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Putin conscripts 160K men as Russia eyes Ukraine offensive
item.description: Russia's largest military draft in 14 years sees 160,000 men called up as Putin plans a spring offensive on Ukraine. Despite stalled U.S. ceasefire talks, the Kremlin insists conscripts won't fight.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Reporter's Notebook: Aftershock from a political 'earthquake' as Le Pen barred from presidential run in 2027
item.description: Le Pen called her ban from running a "nuclear bomb" by the system, after a court convicted her of embezzlement and blocked her from office for five years.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Netanyahu accuses Israeli police of trying to 'topple' his government
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu is outraged over the ongoing Qatar probe and says it is a political investigation that is meant to prevent the firing of Shin Bet chief Ronen Bar.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Amsterdam knife attacker who injured 2 Americans suspected of having 'terrorist motive,' prosecutors say
item.description: The Ukrainian suspect who stabbed five people in Amsterdam last week, including two Americans, likely had a "terrorist motive," Dutch prosecutors say.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump wants new Iran nuclear deal after he withdrew from the first
item.description: Trump withdrew the U.S from the deal during his first term, claiming it was “the worst and most one-sided transaction.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, NewsNation

item.title: Middle East latest: Israel prepares to move deeper into Gaza and orders Rafah evacuated
item.description: The Israeli military indicated on Monday it could soon launch another major offensive in Rafah and ordered most of the southernmost city in the Gaza Strip evacuated. Israel ended its ceasefire with Hamas and renewed its air and ground war earlier this month.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Israeli military orders evacuation of most of Gaza's southern city of Rafah
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — The Israeli military on Monday issued sweeping evacuation orders covering most of Rafah, indicating it could soon launch another major ground operation in the southernmost city in the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Pakistan plans to expel 3 million Afghans from the country this year
item.description: Pakistan plans to expel 3 million Afghans from the country this year, as a deadline for them to voluntarily leave the capital and surrounding areas expired on Monday.
item.dc:creator: RIAZ KHAN and RIAZAT BUTT, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Smell of death permeates Myanmar cities after quake kills over 1,600, leaves others buried
item.description: The 7.7 magnitude quake hit midday Friday with an epicenter near Mandalay, bringing down scores of buildings and damaging other infrastructure like the city’s airport.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: The Latest: Countries sending humanitarian aid after Myanmar earthquake
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — The death toll from Myanmar's powerful 7.7 magnitude earthquake keeps climbing amid rescue efforts. The military government said Saturday that 1,644 people have been killed, with thousands of others injured and dozens missing.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Myanmar's earthquake death toll jumps to 1,644 as more bodies are recovered from the rubble
item.description: Myanmar’s ruling military confirmed death toll from a devastating 7.7 magnitude earthquake rose to 1,644, as more bodies were pulled from the rubble of the scores of buildings that collapsed.
item.dc:creator: David Rising, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Doctor cites the pope's 'surprising improvement' after surviving life-threatening crises
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis has shown ‘’a truly surprising improvement’’ since returning to the Vatican to convalesce after surviving a life-threatening bout with double-pneumonia, the doctor who coordinated the pontiff’s five-week hospitalization said Saturday. “I find him very lively,’’ Dr.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN BARRY and TRISHA THOMAS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Massive quake rocks Myanmar and Thailand. Hundreds feared dead
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — A powerful earthquake rocked Myanmar on Friday, causing extensive damage across a wide swath of one of the world’s poorest countries and prompting officials to warn that the initial death toll — above 140 — was likely to grow in the days ahead.
item.dc:creator: DAVID RISING and JINTAMAS SAKSORNCHAI, Associated Press, NewsNation
